info.description:
REST services for managing Formulations

To reinforce the security, from now on, the authoring web services require the header X-Requested-With. Its value must be XMLHttpRequest or script.
info.title: Formulations Web Services

post.description: Creates a new Single Piece Description

get.summary: Get recipe data in a format that can be exported to an external system
get.description: Returns recipe in a format that can be exported to an external system

get.summary: Get a formula that is calculated view of recipe along with constituency and materials views given formula id

parameters.name: billOfSubstance
parameters.description: List of all ingredients that represents the constituents and pure materials in a formula

parameters.name: billOfMaterial
parameters.description: List of all ingredients that represents materials the formula.

parameters.name: expandMaterials
parameters.description:
Expands formulated materials while listing the bill of materials within the formula.
When false, the billOfMaterials will contain Materials and Formulated Materials that are used directly in the formula.
When true, the billOfMaterials array will expand Formulated Materials and will replace them with ingredients from the related formula.

post.summary: Imports a recipe from external system
post.description: Imports recipe from external system and creates corresponding Formulated Material in Materials service

patch.description: Enable changing specific recipe metadata properties

get.summary: Get the full list of Recipe Classifications
get.description: Fetches the full list of Recipe Classifications

patch.description: Update values on a Recipe Classification with the given ID

top.name: $top
top.description:
Optional OData parameter that specifies how many items to return. 

 Example $top=10 will return 10 items

skip.name: $skip
skip.description:
Optional OData parameter that specifies how many items to skip from the start of the items to return. 

Example $skip=7 will skip the first 7 items from the results

orderby.name: $orderby
orderby.description:
Optional OData parameter that specifies the order of the items returned. 

 Example $orderby=name desc will order the results by name in descending order

filter.name: $filter
filter.description:
Optional OData parameter that specifies a filter for the items to return. 

 Example $filter=contains(tolower(prefLabel)=tolower('ab')).

batchSize.description: Primary batch size for the recipe

dateCreated.description: The date that the formula was created in external system

costPerKg.description: Cost represents the Cost per Kg of this raw material. The Cost property is either entered by the user, or set by the Cost Driver.  The Cost Driver can be configured to obtain cost information from several databases, of which the raw material database could be one.

dateCreated.description: The date that the formula was created in external system

lastUpdated.description: The date that the formula was last updated

costPerKg.description: Cost represents the Cost per Kg of this raw material. The Cost property is either entered by the user, or set by the Cost Driver.  The Cost Driver can be configured to obtain cost information from several databases, of which the raw material database could be one.

isBatchSizeFloats.description: Indicates that the total batch size should be calculated from the sum of all of the ingredients and not from the target batch size set by the user

isPieceSizeFloats.description: Indicates the formula piece size changes dynamically

recipeNumber.description: Number used to identify the recipe

recipePurpose.description: The reason that user enters for tracking of each edit

state.description: Current maturity state of imported recipe

hasProductForm.description: Physical form representation of a formula

targetSpecificGravity.description: Target specific gravity is defined as the ratio of the density of a given material, to the density of water. Substances with a specific gravity greater than 1 are heavier than water, and those with a specific gravity of less than 1 are lighter than water.

useBatchType.description: The source of the batch size information

productionApprovedDate.description: The date that the recipe was approved for production

overage.description: The percentage of the formulated material that will be lost when making a batch.

isIntermediateFormula.description: Indicates if the recipe is not for market production

nominalConcentration.description: When a formula is used in another formula as composite, its target ingredient QS level  (Actual QS By Type Level 8) is stored as QS By Type Original Level 8

includeOverage.description: Include the overage amount in the batch size

lastUpdated.description: The date that the formula was last updated

dateCreated.description: The date that the formula was created in the system

isPieceSizeFloats.description: Indicates the formula piece size changes dynamically

isBatchSizeFloats.description: Indicates that the total batch size should be calculated from the sum of all of the ingredients and not from the target batch size set by the user

costPerKg.description: Cost represents the Cost per Kg of all the raw material.

recipePurpose.description: The reason that user enters for tracking of each edit

recipeNumber.description: Number used to identify the formula

isRescaleWeights.description: Determines if the ingredient weights to rescale when you change the batch size. Formulation Design recalculates the ingredients' weights so that their ratio and percentages remain the same in the new batch size. This is the default rescale behavior.

singlePieceSize.description: Primary single piece size on the formula

singlePieceSizeUnit.description: Units set for single piece size

singlePieceSizeDescription.description: Physical form characterization of the single piece size

producesMaterial.description: The corresponding formulated material informations when the formula is in reviewed state.

targetSpecificGravity.description: Target specific gravity is defined as the ratio of the density of a given material, to the density of water. Substances with a specific gravity greater than 1 are heavier than water, and those with a specific gravity of less than 1 are lighter than water.

useBatchType.description: The source of the batch size information. If weight (useWeight), if volume based (useVolume) and if piece based (usePiece).

batchSize.description: Primary batch size for the recipe

batchSizeWeightDry.description: The total batch size weight of the Formula after processing (Dry)

createdBy.description: The name of the person who created the formula.

hasProductForm.description: Physical form representation of a formula

activity.description: The measure of the percentage of the active or target material in a formula. For composites, the Activity field will be calculated based on the percentage of the Target material in a composition.  You can edit this value to change the level of the target materials within the composition. The activity for composite materials can be changed only if the composition has at least one ingredient identified as a Target. Activity calculations are based on on-label ingredients and off label ingredients are ignored.

lossOnProcessing.description: The amount of ingredient that is lost during processing of the formula; for example, boiling or baking through steam or evaporation. This value represents the difference between the Wet % and Dry% columns. The loss is tracked so the final amount is known. After the processing loss value is entered, the formula recalculates the other ingredients to make up for the loss.

costOfPiece.description: Total cost of one piece of this formula.

targetPercentActive.description: The target percentage value to determine the amount of formula used in the consuming formula.

targetPercentWet.description: Total of targetPercentWet of all ingredients in the formula.

targetPercentDry.description: The percent of all the  ingredients in the formula the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetPercentActiveDry.description: The calculated percent of all the material components added to a formula, after processing loss,measured on an activity basis.

targetWeightActive.description: The calculated quantity of all the material components added to a formula on an activity basis

targetWeightWet.description: Total of targetWeightWet of all ingredients in the formula

targetWeightDry.description: The weight of all the ingredients in the formula the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetWeightActiveDry.description: The system-calculated quantity of all the raw material component added to a formula, after processing, measured on an activity basis.

totalWeightAddedToFormulaDry.description: The total weight of all ingredients or constituents that is delivered to the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration.

totalPercentAddedToFormulaDry.description: The percentage of an ingredient or constituent in the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration. For constituents, this is different from the Dry %, which is their percentage in the parent ingredient.

iUPotencyUnits.description: A picklist of all units that can be selected including weights and volumes

overallPercent.description: The percentage of all the ingredient that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Wet %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentDry.description: The percentage of all the ingredients the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Dry %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentInFormula.description: The percentage of all the ingredients or constituents that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

overallPercentInFormulaDry.description: The percentage of all the ingredients or constituents that the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

moles.description: The amount of a material based on Avagadro’s number (6.022 × 10²³)

orderingWeight.description: The total weight of all the ingredients that you intend to procure.Formulation Design calculates this by adding the overage to the weight weight. You can edit this value from the calculated value.

orderingPercent.description: The percentage of all the ingredients that you intend to procure.

perPieceOrderingWeight.description: The amount of all the ingredients to order from stock for one sales unit of the formula. This value is formula-specific, and is not in the materials database.

orderingWeightAddedToFormula.description: The net procured amount of all the ingredients that is delivered in the formula.

orderingCostInFormula.description: The cost contribution of all the ingredients to the cost of the total procured amount of the batch.

orderingPercentCostInFormula.description: The percentage contribution of all the ingredients towards the total ordering cost of the formula. This is based on the Ordering Cost in Formula.

orderingCostPerPiece.description: The cost contribution of the procured amount of all the ingredients to the total cost of the batch.

targetPercentWetWeightPerWeight.description: The percentage of all the ingredients that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentWetWeightPerVolume.description: The percentage of all the ingredients that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as volume.

targetPercentDryWeightPerWeight.description: The Dry % w/w is the ratio of the weight of all the ingredients to the weight of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetPercentDryWeightPerVolume.description: The Dry % w/w is the ratio of the weight of all the ingredients to the volume of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetPercentActiveWeightPerWeight.description: The active percentage of all the ingredients that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentActiveWeightPerVolume.description: The active percentage of all the ingredients that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as volume.

targetPercentActiveDryWeightPerWeight.description: The Dry % w/w is the ratio of the weight of all the ingredients to the weight of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration for the active portion of the ingredient.

targetPercentActiveDryWeightVolume.description: The Dry % w/w is the ratio of the weight of all the ingredients to the volume of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration for the active portion of the ingredient.

state.description: Current maturity state of imported recipe

isScaleIngredientsToBatchSize.description: Formulation Design recalculates the ingredients' weights so that their percentages remain unchanged in the new phase size. This is the default rescale behavior.

isLockCompositionForPerPieceChange.description: When this option is selected, changing an ingredient's per-piece level in the premix phase also changes the levels of other ingredients in the premix, so that the ratio of ingredients in the premix stays the same. The levels of links to the premix update to reflect the new absolute amounts of ingredients in the premix. For more information, see "Working with Per-Piece Quantities in Premixes."

isFixedSize.description: Select this option to keep the phase weight constant, and type the phase's total weight in the box. The percentages of ingredients in the phase are relative to this weight.

includedInFormulaTotal.description: Determines whether a phase is a premix phase or not. Its ingredients can be included in the formula totals by means of a Link to Premix row in the formula grid. The phase itself is excluded from the formula's totals. For more information, see "About Phases" and "Creating a Premix Phase."

rowNumber.description: Row Number in the Formula Grid
Row Number of the processing instruction at formula grid
Row Number of the ingredient at the formula grid

targetQuantityWet.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula.

targetQuantityWetUnit.description: The weight or volume units, selected by the formulator, in which to display the ingredient quantity.

processingLoss.description: Loss which can happen on ingredient on processing

ingredientActivity.description: The amount of the ingredient that is lost during processing; for example, through steam or evaporation during boiling or baking. This value represents the difference between the Wet % and the Dry % values. The loss is recorded in this column, so that the final amount is known. After you enter a processing loss, Formulation Design recalculates the other ingredients to compensate for the loss.

ingredientType.description: The ingredient type described in three categories - Adjustable, Standard Tolerance, Weight Critical.

costPerKg.description: Cost per Kilogram for an ingredient

overage.description: The percent excess (or "overage") of a batch that is made to compensate for processing losses

targetQuantityWet.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula.

targetQuantityWetUnit.description: The weight or volume units, selected by the formulator, in which to display the ingredient quantity.

processingLoss.description: The amount of the ingredient that is lost during processing; for example, through steam or evaporation during boiling or baking. This value represents the difference between the Wet % and the Dry % values. The loss is recorded in this column, so that the final amount is known. After you enter a processing loss, Formulation Design recalculates the other ingredients to compensate for the loss.

ingredientActivity.description: Denotes whether the ingredient is participating in the formulations or not

ingredientType.description: The ingredient type described in three categories - Adjustable, Standard Tolerance, Weight Critical.

specificGravity.description: User-entered estimate of the formula's intended specifc gravity. This is used to calculate between weight and volume batch sizes.

targetPercentActive.description: The system-calculated percent of a raw material component added to a formula on an activity basis.

targetPercentWet.description: The user-entered intended percent of a raw material component added to a batch

targetPercentDry.description: The percent of this ingredient the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetPercentActiveDry.description: The percent of this ingredient, on an active basis, the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetWeightActive.description: The weight of active ingredient the formulator intends to be added to the batch.

targetWeightDry.description: The weight of this ingredient the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetWeightActiveDry.description: The weight of this ingredient, on an active basis, the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

totalWeightAddedToFormula.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula.

totalPercentAddedToFormula.description: The percentage of an ingredient or constituent in the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration. For constituents, this is different from the Dry %, which is their percentage in the parent ingredient.

targetConcentration.description: The total wet percentage of the target constituents in a composite ingredient, relative to the ingredient's entire composition.

overallPercent.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Wet %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentDry.description: The percentage of the ingredient the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Dry %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentInFormula.description: The percentage of the ingredient or constituents that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

overallPercentInFormulaDry.description: The percentage of the ingredient or constituents that the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

totalPercentAddedToFormulaDry.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration.

displayRawMaterialNumber.description: Raw Material Number which is displayed

targetPercentWetWeightPerWeight.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentWetVolumePerWeight.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with the ingredient amount (the numerator) measured as volume, and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentDryWeightPerWeight.description: The Dy % w/w is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the weight of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetPercentDryVolumePerWeight.description: The Dry % w/v is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the volume of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration

targetPercentActiveWeightPerWeight.description: The active percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentActiveVolumePerWeight.description: The active percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with the ingredient amount (the numerator) measured as volume, and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentActiveDryWeightPerWeight.description: The Dy % w/w is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the weight of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration for the active portion of the ingredient

targetPercentActiveDryVolumePerWeight.description: The Dry % w/v is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the volume of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration for the active portion of the ingredient

weightCriticalFlag.description: If selected, the ingredient is weight critical, and the weight tolerance is set to 0.5%

standardToleranceFlag.description: If selected, the ingredient has default tolerance which is 1%

qsToPhaseFlag.description: When selected, the ingredient is a QS ingredient for a phase. Its level equals the difference between the weight sum of the other ingredients in the phase, and the total weight of the phase.

qsToPiece.description: When selected in a formula with piece weights, the ingredient is the dry weight filler. The weight of the ingredient automatically adjusts to fill the difference between the sum of the rest of the ingredient weights, and the piece weight. If more than one ingredient has this option selected, they are combined in proportion to make up the difference, so that the ratio between the QS ingredients remains constant.

qsToTarget.description: When selected, this represents the QS to the defined nominal or lot concentration.

isTargetIngredient.description: Denotes that the ingredient is a primary ingredient in the Formula

includeInOffLabelTotals.description: Denotes that the ingredient doesn't contribute to the desired composition of the Formula

substanceNumber.description: Substance Number for the constituent of the material

casNumber.description: Unique number given to all chemical compounds by the Chemical Abstract Service

inciName.description: INCI names (International Nomenclature Cosmetic Ingredient) are systematic names internationally recognized to identify cosmetic ingredients.

dryActivity.description: The activity of the ingredient after processing losses are taken into consideration.

materialNumber.description: A number used by the company to identify and track all general lots of this ingredient.

specificGravity.description: User-entered estimate of the formula's intended specifc gravity. This is used to calculate between weight and volume batch sizes.

targetPercentActive.description: The percent of active ingredient the formulator intends to be added to the batch.

targetPercentWet.description: The percent of ingredient the formulator intends to be added to the batch.

targetPercentDry.description: The system-calculated percent of a raw material component remaining in a formula after processing

targetPercentActiveDry.description: The percent of this ingredient, on an active basis, the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetWeightActive.description: he weight of active ingredient the formulator intends to be added to the batch.

targetWeightDry.description: The weight of this ingredient the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetWeightActiveDry.description: The system-calculated quantity of a raw material component added to a formula, after processing,measured on an activity basis.

orderingWeight.description: The total weight of the ingredient that you intend to procure. Formulation Design calculates this by adding the overage to the weight weight. You can edit this value from the calculated value.

orderingPercent.description: The percentage of the ingredient that you intend to procure.

totalWeightAddedToFormula.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula.

totalPercentAddedToFormula.description: The percentage of an ingredient or constituent in the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration. For constituents, this is different from the Dry %, which is their percentage in the parent ingredient.

targetPercent.description: The sum of the percentage added to the formula of all target constituents in a composite ingredient.

targetConcentration.description: The total wet percentage of the target constituents in a composite ingredient, relative to the ingredient's entire composition.

costInFormula.description: The cost contribution of this material to the total cost of this batch.

percentCostInFormula.description: The percent cost contribution of this ingredient to the total cost of this batch.

costPerPiece.description: The cost contribution of this material to the total cost of one sales unit of this batch.

orderingWeightAddedToFormula.description: The net procured amount of the ingredient that is delivered in the formula.

orderingCostInFormula.description: The cost contribution of the ingredient to the cost of the total procured amount of the batch.

orderingPercentCostInFormula.description: The percentage contribution of the ingredient towards the total ordering cost of the formula. This is based on the Ordering Cost in Formula.

orderingCostPerPiece.description: The cost contribution of the procured amount of the ingredient to the total cost of the batch.

overallPercent.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Wet %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentDry.description: The percentage of the ingredient the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Dry %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentInFormula.description: The percentage of the ingredient or constituents that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

overallPercentInFormulaDry.description: The percentage of the ingredient or constituents that the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

totalWeightAddedToFormulaDry.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration.

totalPercentAddedToFormulaDry.description: The percentage of an ingredient or constituent in the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration. For constituents, this is different from the Dry %, which is their percentage in the parent ingredient.

minimumActualWeightWet.description: Lowest amount to be added to a formula before tripping the “weight out of range�? alert used in batching.

maximumActualWeightWet.description: Greatest percentage of this ingredient that can be used in a production formula due to regulatory constraints.

targetPercentWetVolumePerWeight.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with the ingredient amount (the numerator) measured as volume, and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

After you enter a processing loss, Formulation Design recalculates the other ingredients to compensate for the loss.

targetPercentWet.description: The percent of ingredient the formulator intends to be added to the batch

targetPercentDry.description: The system-calculated percent of a raw material component remaining in a formula after processing

targetPercentActiveDry.description: The percent of this ingredient, on an active basis, the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetWeightActive.description: The weight of active ingredient the formulator intends to be added to the batch.

targetWeightDry.description: The weight of this ingredient the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetWeightActiveDry.description: The system-calculated quantity of a raw material component added to a formula, after processing, measured on an activity basis.

orderingWeight.description: The total weight of the ingredient that you intend to procure. Formulation Design calculates this by adding the overage to the weight weight. You can edit this value from the calculated value.

orderingPercent.description: The percentage of the ingredient that you intend to procure.

totalWeightAddedToFormula.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula.

totalPercentAddedToFormula.description: The percentage of an ingredient or constituent in the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration. For constituents, this is different from the Dry %, which is their percentage in the parent ingredient.

orderingWeightAddedToFormula.description: The total wet percentage of the target constituents in a composite ingredient, relative to the ingredient's entire composition.

overallPercent.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Wet %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentDry.description: The percentage of the ingredient the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Dry %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentInFormula.description: The percentage of the ingredient or constituents that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

overallPercentInFormulaDry.description: The percentage of the ingredient or constituents that the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

totalWeightAddedToFormulaDry.description: The name that a ingredient is branded with by a supplier, vendor or distributor.

totalPercentAddedToFormulaDry.description: The percentage of an ingredient or constituent in the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration. For constituents, this is different from the Dry %, which is their percentage in the parent ingredient.

targetPercentWetWeightPerWeight.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentWetVolumePerWeight.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with the ingredient amount (the numerator) measured as volume, and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentDryWeightPerWeight.description: The Dry % w/w is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the weight of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetPercentDryVolumePerWeight.description: The Dry % w/v is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the volume of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration

targetPercentActiveWeightPerWeight.description: The active percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentActiveVolumePerWeight.description: The active percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with the ingredient amount (the numerator) measured as volume, and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentActiveDryWeightPerWeight.description: The Dry % w/w is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the weight of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration for the active portion of the ingredient

targetPercentActiveDryVolumePerWeight.description: The Dry % w/v is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the volume of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration for the active portion of the ingredient

weightCriticalFlag.description: If selected, the ingredient is weight critical, and the weight tolerance is set to 0.5%

standardToleranceFlag.description: If selected, the ingredient has default tolerance which is 1%

qsToPhaseFlag.description: When selected, the ingredient is a QS ingredient for a phase. Its level equals the difference between the weight sum of the other ingredients in the phase, and the total weight of the phase.

qsToPiece.description: When selected in a formula with piece weights, the ingredient is the dry weight filler. The weight of the ingredient automatically adjusts to fill the difference between the sum of the rest of the ingredient weights, and the piece weight. If more than one ingredient has this option selected, they are combined in proportion to make up the difference, so that the ratio between the QS ingredients remains constant.

qsToTarget.description: When selected, this represents the QS to the defined nominal or lot concentration.

isTargetIngredient.description: Denotes that the ingredient is a primary ingredient in the Formula

includeInOffLabelTotals.description: Denotes that the ingredient doesn't contribute to the desired composition of the Formula

casNumber.description: Unique number given to all chemical compounds by the Chemical Abstract Service

inciName.description: INCI names (International Nomenclature Cosmetic Ingredient) are systematic names internationally recognized to identify cosmetic ingredients.

ingredientActivity.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula.

ingredientType.description: The ingredient type described in three categories - Adjustable, Standard Tolerance, Weight Critical.

overage.description: The percent excess (or "overage") of a batch that is made to compensate for processing losses

dryActivity.description: The activity of the ingredient after processing losses are taken into consideration.

specificGravity.description: User-entered estimate of the formula's intended specifc gravity. This is used to calculate between weight and volume batch sizes.

targetPercentActive.description: The percent of active ingredient the formulator intends to be added to the batch.

targetPercentWet.description: The percent of ingredient the formulator intends to be added to the batch.

targetPercentDry.description: The percent of this ingredient the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration

targetPercentActiveDry.description: The system-calculated percent of a raw material component added to a formula, after processing,measured on an activity basis.

targetWeightActive.description: The system-calculated quantity of a raw material component added to a formula on an activity basis.

targetWeightDry.description: The system-calculated quantity of a raw material component remaining in a formula after processing

targetWeightActiveDry.description: The weight of this ingredient, on an active basis, the formulator wants the batch to contain after processing losses are taken into consideration.

orderingWeight.description: The total weight of the ingredient that you intend to procure.Formulation Design calculates this by adding the overage to the weight weight. You can edit this value from the calculated value.

orderingPercent.description: The percentage of the ingredient that you intend to procure.

totalWeightAddedToFormula.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula.

totalPercentAddedToFormula.description: The percentage of an ingredient or constituent in the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration. For constituents, this is different from the Dry %, which is their percentage in the parent ingredient.

targetPercent.description: The sum of the percentage added to the formula of all target constituents in a composite ingredient.

targetConcentration.description: The total wet percentage of the target constituents in a composite ingredient, relative to the ingredient's entire composition.

orderingWeightAddedToFormula.description: The net procured amount of the ingredient that is delivered in the formula.

overallPercent.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Wet %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentDry.description: The percentage of the ingredient the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration. If the ingredient is a QS ingredient, and the formula includes off-label ingredients, the value in this column is less than the Dry %, because it accounts for the off-label ingredients.

overallPercentInFormula.description: The percentage of the ingredient or constituents that the formula includes when off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

overallPercentInFormulaDry.description: The percentage of the ingredient or constituents that the formula includes when processing losses and off-label ingredients are taken into consideration.

totalWeightAddedToFormulaDry.description: The weight of an ingredient or constituent that is delivered to the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration.

totalPercentAddedToFormulaDry.description: The percentage of an ingredient or constituent in the formula after processing losses are taken into consideration. For constituents, this is different from the Dry %, which is their percentage in the parent ingredient.

minimumActualWeightWet.description: Lowest amount to be added to a formula before tripping the “weight out of range�? alert used in batching.

maximumActualWeightWet.description: Greatest percentage of this ingredient that can be used in a production formula due to regulatory constraints.

targetPercentWetWeightPerWeight.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentWetVolumePerWeight.description: The percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with the ingredient amount (the numerator) measured as volume, and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentDryWeightPerWeight.description: The Dry % w/w is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the weight of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration.

targetPercentDryVolumePerWeight.description: The Dry % w/v is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the volume of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration

targetPercentActiveWeightPerWeight.description: The active percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with both the ingredient amount (the numerator) and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentActiveVolumePerWeight.description: The active percentage of the ingredient that the formulator intends to add to the batch, with the ingredient amount (the numerator) measured as volume, and the formula batch amount (the denominator) measured as weight.

targetPercentActiveDryWeightPerWeight.description: The Dy % w/w is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the weight of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration for the active portion of the ingredient

targetPercentActiveDryVolumePerWeight.description: The Dry % w/v is the ratio of the weight of the ingredient to the volume of the batch after processing losses are taken into consideration for the active portion of the ingredient

weightCriticalFlag.description: If selected, the ingredient is weight critical, and the weight tolerance is set to 0.5%

standardToleranceFlag.description: If selected, the ingredient has default tolerance which is 1%

premixFlag.description: Set to denote the materials used in Premix or not

qsToPhaseFlag.description: When selected, the ingredient is a QS ingredient for a phase. Its level equals the difference between the weight sum of the other ingredients in the phase, and the total weight of the phase.

qsToPiece.description: When selected in a formula with piece weights, the ingredient is the dry weight filler. The weight of the ingredient automatically adjusts to fill the difference between the sum of the rest of the ingredient weights, and the piece weight. If more than one ingredient has this option selected, they are combined in proportion to make up the difference, so that the ratio between the QS ingredients remains constant.

isTargetIngredient.description: Denotes that the ingredient is a primary ingredient in the Formula

linkedFormulaId.description: For composite materials, id of the formula for that material.

includeInOffLabelTotals.description: Denotes that the ingredient doesn't contribute to the desired composition of the Formula

casNumber.description: Unique number given to all chemical compounds by the Chemical Abstract Service

inciName.description: INCI names (International Nomenclature Cosmetic Ingredient) are systematic names internationally recognized to identify cosmetic ingredients.

property_hasFormulationType_req.description: Describes the type of a formula.

property_hasFormulationType_resp.description: Describes the type of a formula.
